title: Exos Contract Review — Model Response Schema v1
description: Versioned JSON Schema for the model response produced by both the primary reviewer and the adversarial critic in the Exos contract-review pipeline. This schema is the single validation source of truth for both model passes. Its content_hash is part of the release bundle (see playbooks/schema.json release.output_contract_hash and ARCHITECTURE.md). Any change to this file requires a new output_contract_hash in the release bundle and a legal-governance review. See docs/output-contract.md for the coupling rules.

schema_version.description: Identifies the schema version the model response conforms to. Must equal 'output-schema-v1' for this schema. A mismatch causes the pipeline to route to ERROR_MANUAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

decision.description: The external binary legal decision. ACCEPT = no requested changes identified by tool. REQUEST_CHANGE = one or more issues require attention. There is no third legal category; uncertainty is carried by confidence_state.

confidence_state.description: Internal pipeline confidence, distinct from the external legal decision. OK = normal confidence. LOW_CONFIDENCE = model flagged uncertainty; if a concrete issue exists the decision is still REQUEST_CHANGE; if no concrete issue exists the pipeline routes to MANUAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED status. ERROR_MANUAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED is set by the pipeline on schema-invalid-after-retry or leakage hits — the model must not emit this value directly.

confidence_band.description: System metadata: the low-confidence band for this review, surfaced in the result view pre-download. Framed as a system status (not a legal verdict or a third legal category). null when confidence_state is OK. Set by the pipeline from confidence_state; mirrors the confidence_state signal as a UI-surface label so the result view can render the band before the attorney downloads the redline.

oneOf.description: One of the non-OK system confidence states, rendered as a visible band in the result view. Always a system-status label, never a legal decision.

issues.description: Per-issue list. Empty array for an ACCEPT decision (the model must not invent issues when accepting). For REQUEST_CHANGE, at least one issue must be present.

critic_delta.description: Adversarial-critic output. Null when this response is from the primary reviewer or when the critic found no differences. Present when this response carries critic-only additions, contested replacements, or rationale objections. Structured as a CriticDelta object or null.

verdict_summary.description: ACCEPT-path summary: a brief model-generated narrative of what the counterparty changed and why each change was acceptable under the playbook. Source field for the ACCEPT result view ('no requested changes identified by tool — summary of changes reviewed'). Also present on REQUEST_CHANGE for a high-level narrative alongside the per-issue list. Passes the pre-render leakage scan before being surfaced in the UI or stored in a context accessible to non-admin users. Optional — absent for responses generated before this field was added or when the model omits it. See docs/output-contract.md § ACCEPT summary shape.

oneOf.description: Non-empty summary string. Subject to the leakage scan (same scope as other human-surfaced model prose). Must not disclose counterparty names, precedent deal dates, verbatim precedent text, internal playbook IDs, or system-prompt fragments.

Issue.description: A single flagged issue in a REQUEST_CHANGE response.

section_ref.description: Section number and/or title from the counterparty document (e.g. '8 Limitation on Liability'). Must match the section_ref convention from the standard-form diff anchors.

section_title.description: Human-readable section title for display in the review output.

counterparty_change_summary.description: Brief factual description of what the counterparty changed relative to the Exos standard form. No internal strategy or precedent disclosure.

decision.description: Per-issue decision. Always REQUEST_CHANGE at the issue level (issues are only emitted for changes that require redlining).

external_rationale_for_footnote.description: One or two sentence rationale for the external-facing footnote. Must cite the contract position only (section reference and Exos standard). Must not disclose counterparty names, precedent deal dates, verbatim precedent text, system-prompt fragments, or internal playbook IDs. Enforced by the pre-render leakage scan.

proposed_replacement_text.description: Proposed replacement clause language. Bounded by the topic's replacement_text constraints (mode, max_chars, must_not_introduce). An empty string signals mode='none' (flag only, no replacement proposed). The pipeline enforces topic-level max_chars post-validation.

playbook_topic_id.description: The kebab-case topic id from the active playbook (topics[].id) that governs this issue. Used by the pipeline to look up replacement_text constraints and by the audit table to record topic coverage.

internal_precedent_citation.description: Internal audit-only reference to corpus precedent (clause_id, source document, or null). Stripped from generated .docx footnotes. Must not appear in external output. Retained only in retention-governed confidential storage.

provenance.description: System metadata: identifies which pipeline component produced this issue. One of: 'model' (the LLM primary reviewer flagged this issue), 'critic-added' (the adversarial critic added this issue — not present in the primary output), or 'detector:<rule_id>' (a deterministic hard-rejection rule fired; rule_id is the kebab-case id from the playbook hard_rejections list). This field is framed as system metadata in the result view, never as a third legal category. It enables attorneys to calibrate scrutiny: a deterministic detector fire (near-certain, mechanical) warrants different verification effort than an LLM judgment call (probabilistic). The binary external decision (ACCEPT | REQUEST_CHANGE) is unaffected by provenance — provenance is a trust-calibration signal, not a decision modifier.

CriticDelta.description: The adversarial critic's delta: issues the critic adds, replacements the critic contests, and rationale objections. Preserved in the final result for audit. The critic may not silently overwrite the primary's replacement text; a contested replacement surfaces here rather than being swapped in.

added_issues.description: Issues the critic found that the primary missed.

contested_replacements.description: Primary replacement texts the critic flagged as drifting from the playbook position.

critic_objection.description: Why the critic believes the primary's replacement drifts from the playbook.

critic_suggested_replacement.description: Optional critic suggestion. The deterministic reconciler decides whether to surface this; it does not automatically replace the primary's text.

rationale_objections.description: Rationale weaknesses the critic flagged (e.g. over-flagging, weak reasoning) without necessarily contesting the decision.
